question_text: A thermal camera detects infrared radiation from warm objects. Which one of the following best explains why the camera can see a person in complete darkness?

options.label: A
options.text: Infrared light is brighter than visible light

options.label: B
options.text: Warm objects emit infrared radiation even when there is no visible light

options.label: C
options.text: Infrared waves can reflect off the ground to create images

options.label: D
options.text: The camera converts darkness into visible light using electricity

explanation: All warm objects continuously emit infrared radiation as part of their thermal energy. A thermal camera detects this infrared radiation rather than visible light, so it works in complete darkness.
